title: AIS Agentic Engineering Framework — Brownfield Readiness Assessment
description: JSON Schema 2020-12 for the output of the brownfield repository scanner. Conforms to readiness-assessment.yaml produced by the brownfield.assess prompt.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for this document.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this assessment was generated.

target_repo.description: Path or URL of the repository that was assessed.

overall_score.description: Weighted aggregate readiness score (0–100). Derived from the six dimension scores using the documented weighting formula.

maturity_tier.description: Qualitative maturity classification derived from overall_score. Foundation=0–24, Developing=25–49, Established=50–74, Advanced=75–100.

dimensions.description: Scored assessment of the six evaluation dimensions.

discovered_facts.description: Concrete facts observed in the repository, with attribution.

assumptions.description: Assumptions the assessor made in the absence of verifiable evidence.

gaps.description: Identified gaps between the current state and the framework baseline.

work_management.description: Work management tooling discovery and recommendation.

detected_tools.description: Work management tools detected in the repository (e.g. from workflow files, config, or docs).

recommended.description: Recommended work management provider for framework initialization.

mcp_opportunities.description: MCP server integration opportunities identified during assessment.

modernization_hooks.description: Placeholder modernization observations for Phase 3+ processing.

finops_hooks.description: Placeholder FinOps observations for Phase 3+ processing.

next_steps.description: Ordered list of recommended initialization actions, highest priority first.

completeness.description: Confidence in assessment completeness (0.0–1.0). Lower values indicate the assessor lacked access to key areas.

findings.description: Evidence-based observations for this dimension. Each finding is a single concrete statement.

discovered_fact.description: A concrete, observable fact found in the repository.

category.description: Dimension or area this fact belongs to (e.g. ci_cd, documentation, ai_governance).

fact.description: The observed fact, stated as a single declarative sentence.

source.description: File path or reference that is the evidence for this fact.

gap.description: A gap between current repository state and framework baseline requirements.

area.description: Dimension or area where the gap exists.

description.description: Clear description of what is missing or insufficient.

recommendation.description: Specific recommended action to close the gap.

mcp_opportunity.description: An identified opportunity to integrate an MCP server with this repository's workflow.

tool.description: Name of the MCP tool or server (e.g. github, jira, filesystem).

use_case.description: Specific use case this MCP tool would address in this repository.

hook.description: A placeholder observation reserved for future phase processing.

area.description: The area or subsystem this observation relates to.

observation.description: A concise statement of the observation, to be analyzed in a later phase.
